node.text:
Which would you pick?

Follow @Bulgaria 

In Bulgaria, families hold memorial meals after a person dies.

These take place on the 3rd, 9th, 40th day and later anniversaries.

Food is shared or given to others in memory of the deceased.

The tradition comes from Orthodox Christian customs and focuses on remembrance.

🎥 Unknown | DM for credit

In Bulgaria when someone dies, people gather and eat together after the funeral. It’s not a celebration its just a way for family and friends to be together, support each other and remember the person. Sharing food helps people calm down, talk and deal with the loss.
@paulfitza to each their own, for me there’s a time and place for it. If you’re at the club go ahead shake something, a house party go ahead have fun. But not in the house of god lol
@mike459044 no it’s just sinful and women shouldn’t be showing off their ass in public, would you marry a girl who shakes her ass in public to other dudes? Ofcourse not

@paulfitza like I said you’re online way too much because that literally never happens in the “real” world…. I don’t go to Harris teeters and just see women shaking ass. Go outside, go fishing, go hunting, hiking, anything to renew your mental to normal.
Interesting music choice 🤨
@ng_unick it’s because the woman is black
@ng_unick Guess why?
does that mean people eat you ?
@alec_rhys holy shit 😂😂😂

@lazar_catalin112 its not orthodox nor muslim tradition. Its a turkish tradition comes from central asia and known for more than thousand years. Its probably Ottomans influence in balkan orthodox and middle eastern countries
@omercagridemirezen BS its probably a Indo-European thing since its done in most European countries
Bro what is even the context of the first video 😭😭
They eat dead people in Bulgaria 🇧🇬??
@just.here.for.the.memes.mate noooo, in many cultures across Eurasia, after someone dies the family organises a big feast. It is said to help the soul depart peacefully
@the_dum_reptile ik mate it was sarcasm
@just.here.for.the.memes.mate yes we do and it’s part of our culture so please don’t make fun of it
I would rather touch bulgaria
@overloaded.us Not if Bulgaria touches you first lil ni57ga
Nothing wasted 😤
Thats not considered America. That would be a section 8
But we don’t do that in the rest of South America I don’t know what kind of America do you mean
Which is apart of America
@elaminate your part, possibly. We've balkanized since civil war
@iship61 Monkeydonian national anthem
@gencturk_58 lan 😢👏
Propaganda. The top one is actually an Albanian funeral.
@isaac.landreneau broo
America won this one
@hovru333 cap
Nigga
Are you making food from the dead?
@zakary_art dont u wanna be kebab
@stratmanst I've always wanted to be a shawarma skewer
@vasilije_gligic Romania and Bulgaria share the same tradition, btw, i’m an Romanian guy

Come on now you obviously are not familiar with American traditions at funerals. I have been to many funerals and no one twerked by the coffin. By the way after the funeral we go to eat at restaurant to celebrate the life of the dead too.
Iam a  Bulgarian American and take offense to that. Don’t  put lies out there. Not true period.
Totalmente a favor de um funeral búlgaro @ninachapolittle
@rodrigofsantos7 búlgaros 🤝 nordestinos
Doesn't matter if you are dead
Elite propaganda
